title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness and dating app Hinge is responding with an initiative to support in-person socializing. Hinge announced a $1 million fund for social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable social events as part of a global expansion of its One More Hour social impact initiative.
bullets: Gen Z faces rising loneliness, per the description.
Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Funds target New York, Los Angeles and London.
Grants support free or affordable social events for young people.
This is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour initiative.
takeaways: Description contains no information on grid or data center operations or infrastructure needs.
There are no technical or load-related details in the description that would inform capacity planning.
Grid/data center decision-makers cannot derive actionable impacts from the provided description alone.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent can understand a company's business. The presenters build the pipeline end-to-end and emphasize that no graph expertise is required.
bullets: General-purpose agents can’t inherently understand company-specific information.
Knowledge graphs are presented as the solution to give agents business context.
The session shows automatic construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
The graph is connected to an AI agent to enable domain-aware behavior.
End-to-end demo with no graph expertise required.
takeaways: If you need AI that understands your organization’s data, knowledge graphs can provide that context without requiring staff to learn graph theory.
Automated KG construction can reduce time and specialized skills needed to prepare enterprise data for AI agents.
Connecting a KG to an agent enables an end-to-end solution for business-aware AI capabilities.
Consider the KG vs vector DB tradeoffs (see linked prior episode) when evaluating architectures for enterprise AI.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, outlines that while energy tax credits are the most visible sustainability incentives, a broader set of tax credits and incentives—such as state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation—can also support sustainability initiatives. The core message: viewing sustainability investments through a wider tax lens can unlock meaningful tax value for businesses.
bullets: Energy incentives are commonly top of mind but aren’t the whole picture.
State and local abatements can materially support sustainability projects.
Location-based incentives may influence project siting and economics.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation like reimagined packaging or processes.
A broader tax strategy can extract additional value from sustainability investments.
takeaways: Don't limit tax planning to energy credits—evaluate state/local abatements and location-based incentives when scoping grid or data center projects.
Include R&D tax credit eligibility in innovation efforts (e.g., cooling, process, or systems improvements) to capture additional tax value.
Work with tax advisors to map jurisdiction-specific incentives early in site selection and capital planning.
Factor broader incentive potential into financial models to improve project economics and return on sustainability investments.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The sky is full of stars, and Copilot helps Christa find the star within. The clip closes with a simple call-to-action inviting viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Copilot framed as helping Christa 'find the star within.'
Imagery evokes a sky full of stars—an inspirational tone.
Direct call-to-action: 'Download & try Copilot' via link in bio.
Short, promotional messaging focused on individual empowerment.
takeaways: The messaging emphasizes end-user empowerment—useful when planning adoption and training communications.
A clear, easy CTA (link in bio) suggests making trials and onboarding links highly accessible for faster uptake.
Promotional, person-focused content should be paired with technical deployment materials for enterprise stakeholders.
Inspirational storytelling can be leveraged to increase internal engagement during product rollouts.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… NVIDIA’s GeForce Garage highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build contest, u/JeffersonPutnam, who assembled a small-form-factor system built around a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The description lists the full parts list (chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: RTX 5090 FE; motherboard: MSI MPG B850I Edge; CPU: Ryzen 7 9800X3D; 32GB DDR5-6000; Corsair SF1000; Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) and links to the PCPartPicker build and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Contest winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig.
Host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato).
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker (link provided).
Key hardware: Fractal Design Era 2, RTX 5090 FE, MSI B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS.
GeForce social links and subscription prompts included.
takeaways: The build pairs a flagship GeForce RTX 5090 with a high-performance CPU (Ryzen 7 9800X3D) and 32GB DDR5-6000, illustrating a single-node, workstation-class consumer configuration.

A small-form-factor chassis (Fractal Design Era 2) is used alongside a 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000) and a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS), indicating notable power and cooling provisioning when integrating flagship GPUs into compact systems.
The PCPartPicker parts list link provides exact component choices for evaluating compatibility, power, and thermal considerations at the component level.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions that unlocked entirely new revenue streams. Sreeni Alakappan notes they leveraged GenAI and Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations, boost developer productivity, and compress their digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
Efforts aimed to boost developer productivity.
The partnership unlocked new revenue streams for TTEC.
Consulting support compressed the company’s digital transformation timeline and expanded ambitions.
takeaways: Evaluate cloud consulting to shorten AI deployment timelines and speed time-to-value.
Leverage GenAI and scalable cloud infrastructure to improve developer productivity and operational workflows.
Consider AI-driven initiatives as potential new revenue streams rather than only cost-savings projects.
Use external cloud expertise to expand strategic ambitions while accelerating transformation efforts.
